sprint_goal: Wall screen shows 7-day calendar strip at 1920x1080. PWA installs on Android and iOS. Idle slideshow activates.

agent_instructions.role: You are a senior full-stack engineer building HomeHub from scratch. You write complete, production-quality code. Never produce stubs or TODO placeholders — every file you create must be fully functional. Work through tasks in the exact order listed. A task marked is_blocker:true must have all acceptance_check commands passing before you proceed to the next task. After each task run the commands in acceptance_check before marking it done.

python_environment.policy: ALL Python work must use the project virtual environment at backend/.venv. Never use the system Python or pip directly for project dependencies.
python_environment.verify_active: which python | grep .venv || echo 'ERROR: venv not active'
python_environment.rule: Before running ANY python, alembic, or pytest command, confirm the venv is active. If the shell prompt does not show (.venv), run the activate command first.
python_environment.docker_note: Docker containers have their own isolated Python environment via the Dockerfile — the .venv is for local development and CI only, not inside containers.

node_environment.policy: All Node/npm work runs from the frontend/ directory. node_modules are local to frontend/ — never install globally.
node_environment.rule: Always cd into frontend/ before running npm commands.

code_style.python: PEP8, type hints on all functions, async/await throughout, docstrings on public functions
code_style.typescript: strict mode, explicit return types, no any, functional components with hooks
code_style.general: no commented-out code, no TODO comments, no stubs, complete files every time

agent_instructions.file_rule: Always write the complete file. Never truncate. If a file already exists, read it first then write the full updated version.
agent_instructions.error_rule: All API endpoints must have try/except. All async DB operations wrapped. No unhandled exceptions reach the client.
agent_instructions.version_rule: The application version is stored in ONE canonical location: backend/app/core/config.py as APP_VERSION = '0.1.0'. The Dockerfile, README badge, and API health endpoint all read from this. Never hardcode the version string in more than one place.
agent_instructions.theme_default: dark — the <html> element ships with class='dark' by default. Users can toggle per-profile. Stored in users.settings_json.theme (light|dark).
agent_instructions.user_switch: Shared devices (wall, kitchen tablet) need a quick user-switch. NavShell must include a 'Switch User' button that shows the family member avatar grid and requires PIN to switch — without a full logout/login cycle.
agent_instructions.notifications: Backend must send both email (SMTP) and Discord webhook notifications for: sync job failures, backup job errors, and release announcements. Both channels are wired up via backend/app/services/notifications.py

stack.backend: Python 3.12 + FastAPI + SQLAlchemy 2.0 async + aiosqlite + Alembic + APScheduler + PyJWT + passlib[bcrypt]
stack.frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui + TanStack Query v5 + Zustand + React Router v6 + axios + vite-plugin-pwa + Workbox + react-big-calendar + date-fns
stack.infra: Docker Compose v2 + Caddy 2 alpine + nginx:alpine (frontend prod) + Avahi mDNS
stack.ci: GitHub Actions AMD64 only (no QEMU, no ARM64)

key_decisions.calendar_sync: ICS feed URLs only. No OAuth. Sync upserts on external_uid. External always wins on conflict.
key_decisions.recurring_events: Fully expand recurring events to individual rows on import. 12-month rolling window.
key_decisions.colour_model: Colour on calendar_sources.color_hex. Filter chips per source not per person.
key_decisions.host: AMD64 Linux server. Pi is display-only device pointing at server.
key_decisions.repo: GitHub public. GHCR images. GitHub Actions CI.
key_decisions.dev_machine: Mac Intel + Docker Desktop. homehub.local via Bonjour.

context.db_pattern: UUID TEXT primary keys. All timestamps UTC. Soft deletes via is_deleted. WAL mode + foreign_keys=ON pragmas on connect.
context.auth_pattern: JWT in httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookie. get_current_user FastAPI dependency. PIN rate limit: 5 attempts per 60s window.
context.sync_pattern: APScheduler AsyncIOScheduler. Each calendar_source has sync_interval_hours. Sync job checks source.last_synced_at + interval on each scheduler tick. Upsert on (source_id, external_uid). Delete rows where external_uid no longer in feed.

repo_structure.backend/app/main.py: FastAPI app, lifespan hooks, CORS, routers, StaticFiles mount
repo_structure.backend/app/core/database.py: async engine, WAL+FK pragmas, get_db dependency
repo_structure.backend/app/core/security.py: JWT, bcrypt, get_current_user, require_role, PIN rate limiter
repo_structure.frontend/src/wall/: Wall display components (isolated, no auth guard)

env_vars.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: Discord webhook URL for notifications. Create at: discord.com/developers/applications → your server → Integrations → Webhooks
env_vars.SMTP_PASSWORD: SMTP password or app-specific password. For Gmail generate at: myaccount.google.com → Security → App passwords
env_vars.SMTP_FROM: From address for notification emails e.g. homehub@gmail.com
env_vars.SMTP_TO: Recipient address for admin alerts e.g. your@email.com
env_vars.DATA_PATH: ./data  # Change to NAS mount point e.g. /mnt/nas/homehub
env_vars.APP_VERSION: 0.01  # Do not edit manually — updated by release checklist

execution_rules.rule_1: Complete tasks in the exact order listed. Do not start a task until all tasks in depends_on are verified passing.
execution_rules.rule_2: Tasks marked is_blocker:true must have all acceptance_check commands passing before you proceed.
execution_rules.rule_3: Write every file completely. No stubs, no truncation, no TODOs.
execution_rules.rule_4: After completing ALL tasks in this sprint, run the sprint_exit_verification commands below.

tasks.title: WebSocket endpoint for real-time wall updates

files_to_create.spec: EventBus class with a list of async listener callbacks. subscribe(callback) adds to list and returns an unsubscribe function that removes it. async emit(event_type: str, payload: dict) broadcasts {type, data} to all listeners, removing dead ones on exception.

APIRouter. ConnectionManager class: connect(ws) accepts and appends, disconnect(ws) removes, async broadcast(message dict) sends JSON to all active connections removing failed ones. Module-level manager instance. Module-level event_bus = EventBus(). WS /api/ws/wall: connect the websocket, subscribe manager.broadcast to event_bus, enter receive loop (await websocket.receive_text() to keep alive), on WebSocketDisconnect unsubscribe and disconnect. Import event_bus in calendar service and emit calendar_updated after any event write.

files_to_modify.change: After any successful event create/patch/delete, call await event_bus.emit('calendar_updated', {source_id: ...})

acceptance_check: curl -sk -N 'wss://homehub.local/api/ws/wall' --http1.1 2>&1 | head -5 || echo 'WS endpoint exists — test with wscat if available'
docker compose logs api | grep -i 'websocket\|ws' | tail -3 && echo WS_ROUTE_REGISTERED

tasks.title: Wall display components: layout, clock, 7-day strip, idle mode

files_to_create.spec: Root wall component. Full-screen fixed div covering 100vw 100vh, overflow hidden, dark background (#0f172a), no scrollbars. CSS grid: 2 columns, left column 380px (clock + date + family members panel), right column fills remaining (~1540px) for the 7-day strip. Idle timer: module-level constant IDLE_MS from env or 300000ms default. useEffect attaches touchstart and mousemove listeners calling resetIdle(). resetIdle clears and restarts a setTimeout that sets isIdle=true. When isIdle: render full-screen WallPhotoPlaceholder with clock overlaid bottom-right, click/touch exits idle. Left panel: WallClock at top, below it a WallMemberList showing each family member's name and colour dot. Right panel: WallSevenDayStrip. All text white. No navigation chrome. No auth guard. Auto-refresh calendar data every 60 seconds via TanStack Query refetchInterval.

useState(new Date()), setInterval updating every 1000ms. Display: time as HH:MM using toLocaleTimeString hour 2-digit minute 2-digit hour12 false — font-mono text-8xl font-bold tracking-tight text-white. Below it: full date as weekday long month long day numeric — text-2xl text-slate-400. Props: className optional string.

Fetch events for today through today+6 days using useCalendarEvents with refetchInterval 60000. Also open WebSocket to /api/ws/wall — on calendar_updated message call queryClient.invalidateQueries(['calendar-events']). WebSocket fallback: if WS fails, rely on the 60s polling. Display 7 columns. Each column: header (Mon 5, Tue 6 etc — day abbrev + date number) at top with today highlighted (white bg, dark text vs transparent others). Below header: list of event pills for that day sorted by start_dt. Each pill: coloured left border matching source color_hex, event title truncated, start time. Max 5 pills per day then '+N more' indicator. Columns have equal width filling the container. Text is readable from 2m — min pill font 16px, column header 20px. Column borders: thin slate-700 vertical dividers.

Load family members from useQuery GET /api/users. Display each member as a row: coloured circle (20px diameter, filled with member.color_hex) + display_name text-lg text-slate-300. Stack vertically with 12px gap.
Full-screen div with dark background. Centred HomeHub text in large muted font. Later phases will replace this with a real photo slideshow. Props: fullscreen bool.

files_to_modify.change: Route /wall renders WallLayout directly — no nav wrapper, no auth guard

acceptance_check: Navigate to https://homehub.local/wall at 1920x1080 — no scrollbars, clock visible, 7 day columns visible
Wait idle timeout — screen transitions to placeholder
Touch screen — returns to wall layout
Create an event on /calendar — wall updates within 60 seconds

tasks.title: Dashboard (MVP version) + navigation shell

files_to_create.spec: Role-aware dashboard. Get user from useAuthStore. For all roles: greeting (Good morning/afternoon/evening, {name}). TodaysEventsWidget: useQuery events for today, show as compact agenda list. For admin/co_admin: also show sync status summary (number of sources, any errors). Each widget is in its own Suspense boundary with a skeleton fallback so slow queries don't block others.

Wrapper component with responsive navigation. Mobile (<768px): bottom navigation bar with 4 icons: Home (/dashboard), Calendar (/calendar), Lists (placeholder /lists), Settings (/admin/settings for admin else hidden). Desktop (>=768px): left sidebar 240px with navigation links, app name at top, user avatar + name at bottom with logout button. Does not render when pathname starts with /wall. Active link is highlighted.

files_to_modify.change: Wrap all routes except /wall, /login, /setup in NavShell

acceptance_check: Navigate to https://homehub.local/dashboard — greeting visible, today's events widget loads
Resize browser to 375px wide — bottom navigation bar appears with Home/Calendar icons
Resize to 1024px+ — left sidebar navigation appears

tasks.title: PWA: complete service worker, install prompts, offline banner
tasks.notes: The Caddy cert must be trusted on test devices before this can be tested. See docs/cert-trust.md.

files_to_create.spec: Instructions for trusting the Caddy self-signed cert on: macOS (docker compose exec caddy cat /data/caddy/pki/authorities/local/root.crt > caddy-root.crt, Keychain Access, Always Trust), iOS (serve cert file via python3 -m http.server, Safari download, Settings profile install, Certificate Trust Settings full trust), Pi OS (copy to /usr/local/share/ca-certificates, update-ca-certificates), Android Chrome (device Settings Security install from storage).

acceptance_check: Chrome DevTools Application Manifest — all icons present, display standalone
Chrome on Android — install prompt appears after page load
iOS Safari — tapping Share shows Add to Home Screen option
Turn off server — app loads from cache, yellow offline banner appears
Turn server back on — banner disappears, data refreshes

sprint_exit_verification: All blocker tasks verified with acceptance_check commands
Sprint goal achieved: Wall screen shows 7-day calendar strip at 1920x1080. PWA installs on Android and iOS. Idle slideshow activates.
